GS2.headline: Bill for Synchronisation of State and General Elections Fails in Lok Sabha
GS2.explainer:
• A significant bill proposing the synchronisation of state assembly and general elections (One Nation, One Election) failed to pass in the Lok Sabha.
• The bill aimed to streamline the electoral process, reduce expenditure, and ensure administrative continuity.
• Opposition parties vehemently opposed the bill, citing concerns about federalism, voter choice, and the practical challenges of implementation.
• The failure highlights deep political divisions on major electoral reforms and the difficulty in achieving cross-party consensus on such sensitive issues.
• This outcome signals a setback for the government's long-standing push for 'One Nation, One Election' policy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
prelimsPoints: The bill aimed for 'One Nation, One Election'.
It failed to pass in the Lok Sabha.
Arguments for: reduced expenditure, administrative efficiency, policy continuity.
Arguments against: federalism concerns, voter choice dilution, logistical challenges.
Requires constitutional amendments and broad political consensus.
mainsPoints: Critically examine the arguments for and against the 'One Nation, One Election' proposal in India.
Discuss the implications of synchronised elections on India's federal structure and democratic principles.
Analyze the role of parliamentary consensus and opposition in shaping major legislative reforms.
Suggest alternative electoral reforms that could address issues of cost and administrative burden without compromising federalism.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting the concept of 'One Nation, One Election' with symbols of Lok Sabha and State Assemblies.
GS2.trivia: The idea of simultaneous elections was first proposed by the Election Commission of India in 1983.

GS2.headline: Vice President Jagdeep Dhankhar Wins No-Confidence Motion in Rajya Sabha
GS2.explainer:
• Vice President Jagdeep Dhankhar, serving as the Chairman of the Rajya Sabha, successfully defeated a no-confidence motion tabled by the I.N.D.I.A alliance.
• The opposition alliance moved the motion citing concerns over the Chairman's conduct and alleged partisan rulings during parliamentary proceedings.
• The government and its allies rallied support, emphasizing the constitutional position of the Chairman and defending his actions.
• The motion's failure underscores the government's numerical strength in the Upper House and the challenges faced by the opposition in such parliamentary maneuvers.
• This event highlights the ongoing political tussle and procedural debates within India's parliamentary system.
gsSubTopics: Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
prelimsPoints: Jagdeep Dhankhar is the current Vice President of India and Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.
A no-confidence motion was moved by the I.N.D.I.A alliance.
The motion failed in the Rajya Sabha.
The Chairman presides over the Rajya Sabha sessions.
A no-confidence motion against the Chairman is a rare parliamentary event.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional role and powers of the Vice President as the Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.
Analyze the significance of no-confidence motions in parliamentary democracies and their impact on government-opposition dynamics.
Evaluate the criticisms leveled against presiding officers in Indian Parliament regarding impartiality and procedural fairness.
Suggest reforms to enhance the decorum and effectiveness of parliamentary proceedings in India.
GS2.imageDescription: Vice President Jagdeep Dhankhar presiding over a session in the Rajya Sabha.
GS2.trivia: The Vice President of India is ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha, as per Article 64 of the Indian Constitution.

GS2.headline: India and Japan Hold High-Level Strategic Dialogue, Focus on Indo-Pacific Security
GS2.explainer:
• India and Japan conducted their annual strategic dialogue, focusing on enhancing cooperation in defense, security, and economic sectors.
• Discussions primarily centered on strengthening the Indo-Pacific partnership amidst evolving geopolitical dynamics and regional challenges.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, emphasizing adherence to international law.
• Key areas of collaboration included maritime security, joint military exercises, supply chain resilience, and critical technologies.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of interests between India and Japan as key pillars of stability in Asia.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and Japan held their strategic dialogue.
Focus areas: defense, security, economy, Indo-Pacific.
Both nations committed to a free and open Indo-Pacific.
Discussed maritime security and joint military exercises.
Japan is a key partner in India's 'Act East' policy.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-Japan strategic partnership in ensuring stability and prosperity in the Indo-Pacific region.
Discuss the various dimensions of India-Japan cooperation, including defense, economic, and technological collaboration.
Evaluate how the 'Quad' grouping reinforces bilateral efforts between India and Japan in addressing regional challenges.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in deepening India-Japan ties in the context of changing global geopolitics.
GS2.imageDescription: Foreign ministers of India and Japan shaking hands during a strategic dialogue.
GS2.trivia: India and Japan launched the '2+2' Ministerial Dialogue (Foreign and Defence Ministers) in 2019 to further enhance strategic communication.

GS2.headline: Government Launches Pan-India Digital Literacy Program for Rural Women
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) launched a new pan-India digital literacy program specifically targeting rural women.
• The initiative aims to bridge the digital divide and empower women by equipping them with essential digital skills for everyday tasks and economic opportunities.
• Training modules include basic computer operations, internet usage, digital payments, and accessing government online services.
• The program leverages a network of common service centers (CSCs) and local NGOs for effective outreach and implementation.
• This is part of the government's broader vision for digital inclusion and leveraging technology for socio-economic development.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

Governance, Transparency and Accountability, e-governance- applications, models, successes, limitations, and potential; citizens charters, transparency & accountability and institutional and other measures.
prelimsPoints: Launched by Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY).
Targets rural women across India.
Aims to bridge digital divide and empower women.
Training includes basic computer, internet, digital payments.
Utilizes Common Service Centres (CSCs) for implementation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of digital literacy for women's empowerment and socio-economic development in rural India.
Analyze the challenges in implementing digital literacy programs in rural areas, including infrastructure, accessibility, and cultural barriers.
Evaluate the role of government initiatives like Digital India and CSCs in fostering digital inclusion.
Suggest measures to ensure the sustainability and scalability of such digital literacy programs for long-term impact.
GS2.imageDescription: A group of rural women learning to use tablets and smartphones in a classroom setting.
GS2.trivia: The 'Pradhan Mantri Gramin Digital Saksharta Abhiyan' (PMGDISHA) is another scheme aimed at making 6 crore rural households digitally literate.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Adjourns Hearing on Petitions Challenging Uniform Civil Code (UCC)
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court adjourned the hearing on a batch of petitions challenging the constitutional validity of a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) in a particular state.
• The petitions argue that a UCC infringes upon religious freedom and cultural diversity guaranteed by the Constitution.
• The government has consistently advocated for a UCC, citing Article 44 of the Constitution (Directive Principles of State Policy) which mandates the state to endeavor to secure a UCC for its citizens.
• The debate surrounding UCC involves complex legal, social, and religious considerations, highlighting the tension between uniformity and diversity.
• The adjournment suggests that the court is taking a cautious approach to a highly sensitive and politically charged issue.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court adjourned hearing on UCC petitions.
Petitions challenge constitutional validity of UCC.
Arguments against: infringes religious freedom, cultural diversity.
Arguments for: Article 44, gender justice, national integration.
UCC is a Directive Principle of State Policy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional mandate of the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) under Article 44 and the arguments for its implementation.
Analyze the concerns raised by various communities and legal scholars regarding the potential impact of UCC on religious freedom and cultural diversity.
Examine the socio-political implications of implementing a UCC in a diverse country like India.
Suggest a balanced approach that can reconcile the principles of uniformity and diversity while ensuring gender justice and equality.
GS2.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building in New Delhi.
GS2.trivia: Goa is the only Indian state currently with a Uniform Civil Code, inherited from the Portuguese colonial era.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Indian Polity and Governance
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Directive Principles of State Policy, Fundamental Rights

GS2.headline: Union Cabinet Approves New Policy to Promote Traditional Indian Medicine Systems
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved a new national policy aimed at promoting and integrating traditional Indian medicine systems like Ayurveda, Yoga, Unani, Siddha, and Homoeopathy (AYUSH).
• The policy focuses on research, standardization of practices, quality control of medicines, and mainstreaming AYUSH into public health delivery.
• It seeks to leverage India's rich heritage in traditional medicine for preventive healthcare, wellness, and treating chronic diseases.
• Measures include establishing AYUSH wellness centers, promoting cultivation of medicinal plants, and regulating education and practice.
• This move is expected to boost the AYUSH sector, both domestically and internationally, and provide holistic healthcare options.
gsSubTopics: Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
prelimsPoints: Union Cabinet approved a new policy for traditional Indian medicine systems.
Focuses on AYUSH (Ayurveda, Yoga, Unani, Siddha, Homoeopathy).
Aims for research, standardization, quality control, mainstreaming AYUSH.
Promotes preventive healthcare and wellness.
Includes establishing AYUSH wellness centers.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of traditional Indian medicine systems (AYUSH) in addressing India's healthcare challenges and promoting holistic wellness.
Analyze the steps taken by the government to integrate AYUSH into mainstream public health and the challenges associated with it.
Evaluate the importance of research, standardization, and quality control in enhancing the credibility and global acceptance of AYUSH.
Examine how the promotion of AYUSH can contribute to rural livelihoods through cultivation of medicinal plants and local entrepreneurship.
GS2.imageDescription: Various traditional Indian medicine ingredients and symbols of Ayurveda and Yoga.
GS2.trivia: The Ministry of AYUSH was formed in 2014 to ensure optimal development and propagation of AYUSH systems of healthcare.

GS2.headline: India and Sri Lanka Enhance Maritime Security Cooperation in Indian Ocean
GS2.explainer:
• India and Sri Lanka held high-level discussions to bolster maritime security cooperation in the Indian Ocean region.
• The talks focused on joint patrolling, intelligence sharing, and capacity building to address common challenges like illegal fishing, drug trafficking, and maritime terrorism.
• Both nations emphasized the importance of a free, open, and rules-based maritime order for regional stability.
• This collaboration is crucial for safeguarding strategic sea lanes and ensuring the security of the shared maritime domain.
• The enhanced cooperation reflects the deepening bilateral ties and mutual commitment to regional security.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
keyTerms: SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region)
prelimsPoints: India and Sri Lanka discussed maritime security cooperation.
Focus: Indian Ocean region.
Aims to counter illegal fishing, drug trafficking, maritime terrorism.
Emphasized free, open, rules-based maritime order.
Part of deepening bilateral ties.
mainsPoints: Analyze the strategic importance of Sri Lanka for India's maritime security interests in the Indian Ocean region.
Discuss the various facets of maritime cooperation between India and Sri Lanka and their effectiveness in addressing shared challenges.
Examine the implications of external powers' presence in the Indian Ocean for regional security and India's role.
Suggest measures to further strengthen India-Sri Lanka defense and security partnership, including capacity building initiatives.
GS2.imageDescription: Naval ships of India and Sri Lanka conducting a joint exercise in the Indian Ocean.
GS2.trivia: India and Sri Lanka conduct several joint military exercises, including 'Mitra Shakti' (army) and 'SLINEX' (navy).

GS3.headline: 13 Dead in Mumbai Boat Accident After Navy Speedboat Collides with Ferry
GS3.explainer:
• A tragic accident occurred near Mumbai where an Indian Navy speedboat collided with a passenger ferry, resulting in 13 fatalities.
• The ferry was en route from Mumbai to the popular tourist destination of Elephanta Island when the collision took place.
• Rescue operations were immediately launched by the Indian Navy, Coast Guard, and local authorities, but many passengers were trapped.
• An inquiry has been ordered to ascertain the cause of the collision, focusing on navigation protocols, visibility, and safety standards.
• The incident highlights critical issues in maritime safety, port navigation, and emergency response mechanisms in densely trafficked waterways.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management.
Role of external state and non-state actors in creating challenges to internal security.

Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
prelimsPoints: Accident occurred between Mumbai and Elephanta Island.
Involved an Indian Navy speedboat and a passenger ferry.
Resulted in 13 deaths.
Indian Navy and Coast Guard were involved in rescue.
Inquiry ordered to investigate the cause.
mainsPoints: Analyze the gaps in maritime safety regulations and enforcement in India's coastal and inland waterways, using the Mumbai incident as a case study.
Discuss the challenges faced by disaster management agencies during marine accidents and suggest measures to improve response capabilities.
Evaluate the need for stricter navigation rules and advanced surveillance systems in congested port areas.
Examine the role of technology and training in preventing such accidents in the future.
GS3.imageDescription: Rescue boats and personnel at the site of a boat accident in Mumbai waters.
GS3.trivia: Elephanta Island is famous for its ancient rock-cut caves, which are a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

GS3.headline: Five Suspected Militants Killed in Encounter in Kulgam, J&K
GS3.explainer:
• Security forces conducted an operation in Kulgam district, Jammu and Kashmir, leading to the killing of five suspected militants.
• The encounter followed intelligence inputs about the presence of militants in the area, leading to a cordon and search operation.
• This operation is part of ongoing efforts by security forces to counter terrorism and maintain peace in the region.
• Arms and ammunition were recovered from the encounter site, indicating the militants' intent for subversive activities.
• The incident underscores the persistent security challenges in Jammu and Kashmir, despite a general decline in violence.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: Encounter took place in Kulgam district of Jammu and Kashmir.
Five suspected militants were killed.
Security forces acted on intelligence inputs.
Arms and ammunition were recovered.
Part of ongoing counter-terrorism operations.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving nature of militancy in Jammu and Kashmir and the strategies adopted by security forces to counter it.
Discuss the challenges faced by security forces in counter-insurgency operations, including intelligence gathering and civilian protection.
Examine the socio-economic factors that contribute to radicalization in the region and suggest measures for long-term peace.
Evaluate the effectiveness of current border management and counter-infiltration strategies in J&K.
GS3.imageDescription: Security forces personnel conducting a cordon and search operation in a rural area of Jammu and Kashmir.
GS3.trivia: Kulgam district is located in the Pir Panjal range of the Himalayas, known for its scenic beauty and agricultural output.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate Amidst Persistent Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) concluded its Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, deciding to maintain the repo rate at its current level.
• The decision was influenced by persistent inflationary pressures, particularly in food prices, and global economic uncertainties.
• While GDP growth projections remain robust, the RBI emphasized a cautious approach to ensure price stability and anchor inflation expectations.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to bring inflation within the target band.
• This stance indicates that the RBI is prioritizing inflation control over immediate growth impulses, signaling a watchful wait-and-see approach.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.

Major Crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, Different Types of Irrigation and Irrigation System storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
prelimsPoints: RBI's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) sets the repo rate.
Repo rate was maintained, not changed.
Reason for maintaining: persistent inflation concerns, especially food inflation.
RBI's primary mandate is price stability.
Stance remains 'withdrawal of accommodation'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing RBI's monetary policy decisions, particularly the trade-off between inflation control and economic growth.
Discuss the impact of high food inflation on the Indian economy and the effectiveness of current policy tools to address it.
Evaluate the role of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in maintaining financial stability and its autonomy.
Examine the implications of global economic uncertainties on India's domestic monetary policy.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) comprises six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Earth Observation Satellite
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched an advanced Earth Observation Satellite (EOS) from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre.
• The satellite, carried by a PSLV rocket, is designed to provide high-resolution imagery for various applications, including agriculture, disaster management, and urban planning.
• This mission further enhances India's capabilities in remote sensing and contributes to national development goals.
• The launch demonstrates ISRO's consistent progress in indigenous space technology and cost-effective access to space.
• Data from the satellite will be crucial for environmental monitoring and resource management.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: ISRO launched an Earth Observation Satellite (EOS).
Launch vehicle used: PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle).
Launched from Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota.
Applications: agriculture, disaster management, urban planning.
Enhances India's remote sensing capabilities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Earth Observation Satellites for India's national development and strategic interests.
Analyze ISRO's role in promoting indigenous space technology and making India a leader in cost-effective space missions.
Examine the applications of remote sensing data in various sectors, including climate change monitoring and resource management.
Evaluate the challenges and future prospects for India's space program, including private sector participation.
GS3.imageDescription: A PSLV rocket lifting off from the launch pad, carrying a satellite into orbit.
GS3.trivia: PSLV is one of the most reliable launch vehicles of ISRO, known for its ability to launch satellites into various orbits, including sun-synchronous and geosynchronous transfer orbits.

GS3.headline: India Unveils New National Framework for Climate Change Adaptation
GS3.explainer:
• India's Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched a comprehensive National Framework for Climate Change Adaptation.
• The framework aims to integrate climate resilience into national and sub-national development planning across key sectors.
• It emphasizes a multi-stakeholder approach, involving government agencies, local communities, private sector, and research institutions.
• Key focus areas include water security, sustainable agriculture, ecosystem restoration, and early warning systems for extreme weather events.
• This initiative underscores India's commitment to addressing climate change impacts domestically, following international commitments.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
prelimsPoints: Launched by Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change.
Purpose: integrate climate resilience into development planning.
Focuses on water security, sustainable agriculture, ecosystem restoration, early warning systems.
Emphasizes multi-stakeholder approach.
Aims to address domestic impacts of climate change.
mainsPoints: Discuss the necessity of a national framework for climate change adaptation in India, considering its vulnerability to climate impacts.
Analyze the challenges in implementing climate adaptation strategies across diverse sectors and regions in India.
Evaluate the role of local communities and traditional knowledge in building climate resilience.
Examine how this framework aligns with India's international climate commitments and contributes to global climate action.
GS3.imageDescription: A graphic showing various elements of climate change adaptation, such as sustainable agriculture, water management, and renewable energy.
GS3.trivia: India submitted its first National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC) in 2008, outlining eight national missions.

GS3.headline: Government Announces New PLI Scheme for Renewable Energy Manufacturing
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government announced a new Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme specifically for the renewable energy manufacturing sector.
• This scheme aims to boost domestic manufacturing of solar PV modules, advanced chemistry cell batteries, and green hydrogen components.
• The objective is to reduce India's reliance on imports, create jobs, and establish India as a global manufacturing hub for green technologies.
• Incentives will be provided based on incremental sales and investment in high-efficiency and advanced technology products.
• This move is critical for achieving India's ambitious renewable energy targets and fostering a sustainable energy transition.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: New PLI scheme targets renewable energy manufacturing.
Focuses on solar PV modules, ACC batteries, green hydrogen components.
Aims to reduce imports and create jobs.
Incentives based on incremental sales and investment.
Supports India's renewable energy targets.
mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the government's Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes and their potential impact on India's manufacturing sector.
Discuss how the new PLI scheme for renewable energy manufacturing can contribute to India's energy security and climate goals.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in establishing India as a global manufacturing hub for green technologies.
Examine the role of government incentives in driving private sector investment and technological innovation in critical sectors.
GS3.imageDescription: Solar panels being manufactured in a factory, with a focus on advanced technology.
GS3.trivia: India has a target of achieving 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030.

GS3.headline: India and US Conclude Joint Counter-Terrorism Exercise 'Vajra Prahar'
GS3.explainer:
• The joint military exercise 'Vajra Prahar' between India and the United States concluded, focusing on counter-terrorism operations.
• The exercise involved special forces from both nations, enhancing interoperability and sharing best practices in unconventional warfare.
• Training scenarios included hostage rescue, urban counter-terrorism, and close-quarter combat in simulated environments.
• This annual exercise is a crucial component of the growing defense cooperation between India and the US, aimed at addressing common security threats.
• It reinforces the strategic partnership and mutual commitment to regional and global security.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: Joint military exercise between India and US.
Name of the exercise: 'Vajra Prahar'.
Focus: counter-terrorism operations.
Involved special forces from both countries.
Aims to enhance interoperability and share best practices.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of joint military exercises like 'Vajra Prahar' in strengthening India's defense capabilities and international partnerships.
Analyze how defense cooperation with the US contributes to India's broader security objectives in the Indo-Pacific region.
Examine the challenges and benefits of interoperability between different armed forces in counter-terrorism operations.
Evaluate the role of such exercises in addressing evolving threats posed by global terrorism and regional instability.
GS3.imageDescription: Indian and US special forces personnel engaged in a joint training exercise.
GS3.trivia: The 'Vajra Prahar' exercise is typically held alternately in India and the US.

GS3.headline: India's Q2 GDP Growth Exceeds Expectations, Driven by Manufacturing and Services
GS3.explainer:
• India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth for the second quarter (July-September) of the fiscal year exceeded market expectations.
• The robust growth was primarily driven by strong performance in the manufacturing and services sectors, indicating a broad-based recovery.
• Government capital expenditure and resilient domestic demand also contributed significantly to the positive economic momentum.
• Despite global headwinds and inflationary pressures, the Indian economy has shown remarkable resilience, positioning itself as a fast-growing major economy.
• This positive data provides a boost to investor confidence and reinforces the government's economic policies.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization, of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
prelimsPoints: India's Q2 (July-September) GDP growth exceeded expectations.
Key drivers: manufacturing and services sectors.
Government capital expenditure and domestic demand also contributed.
India remains a fast-growing major economy.
GDP data is released by the National Statistical Office (NSO).
mainsPoints: Analyze the key factors contributing to India's robust GDP growth in the current fiscal year, highlighting the role of manufacturing and services.
Discuss the challenges that India still faces in sustaining high economic growth, such as inflation, global uncertainties, and inequality.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies, including capital expenditure and sectoral reforms, in boosting economic activity.
Examine the implications of strong economic growth for employment generation and poverty reduction in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A chart showing upward trend of India's GDP growth, with economic indicators.
GS3.trivia: India's nominal GDP (current prices) is currently the 5th largest in the world, and its GDP (PPP) is the 3rd largest.

GS3.headline: National Mission on Edible Oils – Oil Palm Shows Promising Progress
GS3.explainer:
• The National Mission on Edible Oils – Oil Palm (NMEO-OP) has shown promising progress in increasing domestic oil palm cultivation.
• Launched to reduce India's heavy reliance on edible oil imports, the mission provides financial assistance and technical support to farmers.
• Expansion of oil palm cultivation is primarily focused in the North-Eastern states and the Andaman & Nicobar Islands, considering their suitable agro-climatic conditions.
• The mission aims to enhance farmers' income and achieve self-sufficiency in edible oils by increasing domestic production.
• Challenges remain in terms of land availability, water management, and ensuring sustainable practices.
gsSubTopics: Major Crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, Different Types of Irrigation and Irrigation System storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: Mission name: National Mission on Edible Oils – Oil Palm (NMEO-OP).
Objective: reduce edible oil imports, increase domestic oil palm production.
Focus regions: North-Eastern states, Andaman & Nicobar Islands.
Provides financial and technical assistance to farmers.
Aims for self-sufficiency in edible oils.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of achieving self-sufficiency in edible oils for India's food security and economic stability.
Analyze the potential benefits and environmental concerns associated with expanding oil palm cultivation in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government missions like NMEO-OP in boosting agricultural production and farmer incomes.
Examine the challenges in implementing large-scale agricultural programs, including land rights, water management, and market linkages.
GS3.imageDescription: A lush oil palm plantation in a tropical region.
GS3.trivia: India is one of the world's largest importers of edible oils, with palm oil accounting for a major share of these imports.
